commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for artifact-backed Telegram proof and the cancel-after-secret credential persistence fix before this draft can merge.; Needs attention: No dependency change was found, but the new secret-entry path can persist provider credentials before the visible Telegram setup flow is committed.; Review finding: [P1] Defer profile writes until setup commits — src/provider-setup/runtime.ts:... (sha=c9f9661960579d78d66cd656e82e46eea157b811)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an owner-only Telegram `/providers` DM setup flow, a provider setup runtime with a private SDK subpath, Telegram secret-reply handling, and provider auth/config replay coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: enter a provider secret, stop at the default-model confirmation, then cancel. `runProviderPluginAuthMethod` has already written auth profiles before the setup runtime reports cancellation.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for artifact-backed Telegram proof and the cancel-after-secret credential persistence fix before this draft can merge.

target.title: Add Telegram provider setup flow
target.body:
Summary
- Add owner-only Telegram `/providers` setup for private DMs.
- Reuse provider auth/profile/config write paths through a private local setup runtime.
- Consume pending secret replies before normal Telegram routing and attempt message deletion.
- Harden setup sessions: expiry, validation retry, plugin policy checks before auth, default-model side effects, and fresh-config patch replay.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/provider-setup/runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-providers.test.ts src/wizard/session.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --pretty false --noEmit`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check` still fails on clean `origin/main`; this PR no longer changes `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`.

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram owner can run `/providers` in a private DM and complete provider auth setup without leaking the API key into normal chat handling.
Real environment tested: local PR-head gateway from isolated worktree, Telegram Desktop DM, bot `@peanuttobot`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `/providers` -> Add or update provider -> xAI -> API key -> pasted fake disposable key -> saved provider -> restarted gateway -> `/providers`.
Evidence after fix: Telegram showed the provider dashboard, deleted the fake key message, returned Provider saved / xAI is ready, and after restart `/providers` showed Default: xai/grok-4.3 and Configured: xAI.
Observed result after fix: The setup flow persisted xAI and did not leave the sensitive key message in the chat.
What was not tested: Real xAI inference with a live key; the key used was fake/disposable. Full Testbox was not rerun after maintainer cleanup.
target.author: obviyus
